mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack reduces consumer spending growth and corporate earnings momentum, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production above 0.5% indicates strengthening manufacturing output, which drives positive short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following strong INDPRO growth, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while CPI inflation remains above 3.0%, real rates tighten aggressively, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods meeting both conditions, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following payroll growth, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.0% annualized, corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, triggering negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of periods where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling unemployment signals a tightening labor-supply bottleneck that boosts near-term corporate pricing power and drives positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
